hard.next_step: Justin to send showpad material in preparation for a disco call and eventual walkthrough of merlin intake
hard.ais_why: USNH is exploring AI capabilities for supplier risk visibility and procurement intake, compelling event framed as 'Exploring AI capabilities to enhance procurement.' Merlin Intake is scoped and the buyer currently runs Jaggaer and Workday, positioning Zycus AI as an additive layer. The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and the step is sending Showpad material for a discovery call, so AI interest is genuine but still early.

north_star_verdict.headline: USNH_Intake_Opp_June'26 is an 11-day-old, auto-qualified $75k Merlin Intake deal that has not activated: the only contact is Jesse Frink (Director of Contracts and Sourcing, an Evaluator, not a champion), no discovery call has happened, LastActivityDate is null, and opportunity-health flags on 2026-06-29 already note no inbound in 14 days. It was moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 2026-06-26, the day after creation, with no buyer dialogue in between, so 'Qualified' is a data step, not buyer validation. Three competitors were logged at creation (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) and UNH already runs Jaggaer as its incumbent per AIS_Why, so this has the shape of an RFP courtesy inclusion rather than a championed deal. The runway to the 2027-02-17 close is long (226 days), so the deal is recoverable, but only if the owner lands the first real buyer conversation in the next 7 days. The single blocker most likely to prevent the close is that we have no economic buyer, no champion, and no discovery, and we are an unproven late-add against an incumbent.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 226 (close 2027-02-17, today 2026-07-06). Created 2026-06-25, qualified 2026-06-26; time in Qualified stage: ~11 days. No submission-date series on record (Qualified/Formal_Eval/Shortlisted submission dates null). Field history shows only Initial Interest to Qualified (2026-06-26) and owner/type stamps, no amount or close-date moves. LastActivityDate null: zero buyer-touching Salesforce activity; the eight 2026-06-29 tasks are rep-side opportunity-health prompts, not buyer engagement. For a ~230-day cycle the required pace is a discovery call and multi-thread inside the first 30 days, RFP positioning by day 60, shortlisting by day 120; on day 11 there is no scheduled discovery and no buyer reply, so the deal is off pace but not yet cold.

Three competitors logged at creation with no discovery context (SF Competitors__c: Coupa;GEP Worldwide;JAGGAER, 2026-06-25); AIS_Why confirms UNH already runs Jaggaer as incumbent

The only next step is rep-side and unfulfilled: 'Justin to send showpad material in preparation for a disco call and eventual walkthrough of merlin intake' with no buyer reply or scheduled meeting (SF Next_Step__c)

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or financial impact has been captured, because no discovery call has occurred. The only signal is AIS_Why (2026-06-25), which frames the driver as 'exploring AI capabilities for supplier risk visibility and procurement intake' and a compelling event of 'Exploring AI capabilities to enhance procurement.' The scoped product (Merlin Intake) implies pain in manual requisition triage and intake routing on top of their Jaggaer/Workday stack, but this is inference from the product, not a buyer-stated or quantified metric (no cycle-time, requisition-volume, compliance, or savings figure exists anywhere). The immediate move is to run the discovery call and anchor it on UNH's current intake pain and a measurable target.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget owner is identified. The single contact role is Jesse Frink, Director of Contracts and Sourcing, logged as Evaluator (SF OpportunityContactRole), which is a process/sourcing seat, not budget authority, and there is no EB or executive-sponsor field populated and no call in which anyone controlled a commercial conversation. Because there are zero Avoma calls and no emails, there is no conversation from which to infer who owns the $75k budget at UNH, so we cannot assign the role without inventing a name. The move is to use the first discovery call to ask Jesse who funds and approves this and to secure an introduction to that person.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are unknown because no discovery, demo, or RFP round has happened yet. The scoped product (Merlin Intake) plus three logged competitors (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) imply UNH is evaluating an AI-enabled procurement-intake capability, and AIS_Why points to 'supplier risk visibility and procurement intake' as the theme, but no buyer has stated what will actually decide the choice (user experience, integration with Jaggaer/Workday, price, public-sector references). The move is to establish the criteria on the discovery call and, critically, learn whether UNH wants an additive AI layer or a suite replacement.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is undiscovered. Field history shows only that the opportunity was created 2026-06-25 and auto-moved Initial Interest to Qualified 2026-06-26 with no preceding buyer step, and the eight 2026-06-29 opportunity-health tasks explicitly note 'Decision Maker is not identified' and 'Get clarity on the evaluation stages and influence the process.' As a US public university, UNH likely runs a formal or RFP-style sourcing process (consistent with a Director of Contracts and Sourcing owning the relationship), but no timeline, approval chain, or committee is confirmed. The move is to map the evaluation stages and the approval chain on the discovery call.
sources: SF Tasks 2026-06-29 (Opportunity Health: 'Decision Maker is not identified')
SF OpportunityContactRole: Jesse Frink, Director of Contracts and Sourcing

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown at this stage and, for an early Qualified deal, appropriately so. UNH is a US public university, so a $75k single-module (Merlin Intake) purchase would typically run as an Order Form plus SOW rather than a full MSA suite deal, and public-sector procurement may require a formal solicitation or cooperative-contract vehicle, but none of this is confirmed (no Current_Contract_Expiration__c, no procurement channel, no InfoSec signal on record). Nothing is required now; the move is to surface the procurement path and any incumbent-contract expiration once discovery opens.
sources: Account is a US public university

identify_pain.narrative: No pain has been confirmed by the buyer. AIS_Why (2026-06-25) records that UNH is 'exploring AI capabilities for supplier risk visibility and procurement intake,' which points to friction in their current Jaggaer/Workday intake experience, but 'exploring' is a curiosity signal, not an articulated operational or financial pain, and there is no owner attached to it. The pain is inferred from the stack and the scoped product, not stated. The move is to have the discovery call convert 'exploring AI' into a specific, owned intake pain (who feels it, how often, what it costs).

champion.narrative: No champion exists. The only contact is Jesse Frink, whose Salesforce role is Evaluator (not Champion or Executive Sponsor) and whose title, Director of Contracts and Sourcing, is a process/procurement seat rather than a business or pain owner. There is zero evidence of advocacy: no Avoma call, no introduction he has made, no internal intel shared, and no reply to the rep's offer to send Showpad material, and the 2026-06-29 health tasks flag single-threading and 'Identify more contact roles.' We do not yet know if Jesse is a coach, a neutral gatekeeper, or simply administering a look. The move is to assess his stance on the first call and, in parallel, find a business/pain owner to develop as champion.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Jesse Frink, Director of Contracts and Sourcing, Evaluator
SF Tasks 2026-06-29 (Opportunity Health: 'Keep the engagement multi-threaded')

competition.narrative: Three competitors are logged in Competitors__c at creation (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, 2026-06-25), and AIS_Why confirms UNH already runs Jaggaer, so Jaggaer is the incumbent and the strongest current threat: it holds the account and can extend its own intake front-end at low switching cost. Coupa and GEP are full-suite alternatives whose bundling advantage (intake inside one platform, one contract) is a real risk against a single-module Merlin Intake sale. Our wedge is that UNH runs Jaggaer and Workday, so Merlin Intake can be positioned as an additive AI intake layer on top of the existing stack rather than a rip-and-replace, which no buyer has yet heard because there has been no discovery call. Nothing here is buyer-stated preference: no competitor is confirmed as leading beyond Jaggaer's incumbency.
sources: SF AIS_Why__c (buyer runs Jaggaer and Workday)

deal_movement.summary: The last real buyer touch is none: LastActivityDate is null and there are no Avoma calls or emails on record. All movement is internal/administrative in the first four days after creation, and the sole next step is a rep-side send that has not been fulfilled or answered.

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is the incumbent, JAGGAER: AIS_Why (2026-06-25) confirms UNH already runs Jaggaer, so it holds the account and can extend its own intake at low switching cost. Coupa and GEP Worldwide (both logged 2026-06-25) are full-suite alternatives whose one-vendor bundling is a real risk against a single-module Merlin Intake sale. No competitor is buyer-confirmed as preferred, because no discovery has happened. Our unactivated wedge: position Merlin Intake as an additive AI layer on the existing Jaggaer/Workday stack, not a rip-and-replace.

The one inferred fit point (an AI intake layer on their existing Jaggaer/Workday stack) is unvalidated.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a fresh, single-threaded, un-discovered deal against an incumbent; the win strategy is to convert a possible courtesy-inclusion into a real evaluation by landing the first buyer conversation, multi-threading past the evaluator, and framing Merlin Intake as additive to the Jaggaer stack.
flags: Land the discovery call now: nothing about pain, criteria, budget, or process is known, and the disco call is the single make-or-break to prove this is a real deal, not a logged look.
Break single-threading: Jesse Frink is an Evaluator, not a champion or budget owner; reach a business/pain owner and the budget authority above him before positioning price.
Win against the Jaggaer incumbent by additive positioning: UNH already runs Jaggaer and Workday, so lead with Merlin Intake as an AI layer on top, not a suite rip-and-replace versus Coupa/GEP.

items.name: Jesse Frink

We have never had a two-way conversation with him, so his stance (supportive, neutral, or gatekeeping) and his access to the economic buyer are unknown. If he is only administering a look and we cannot get introductions to a business/pain owner and budget authority, we stay single-threaded in a price-driven look against an incumbent.

champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Developing a champion, and finding a business/pain owner to be one, is the top priority for the next 14 days.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The AI story (Merlin Intake as agentic procurement intake) is scoped and matches UNH's stated curiosity about AI for supplier risk and intake, but it is entirely unactivated: no discovery, no demo, and the Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set. Positioning is weak by default, not because of buyer resistance.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: USNH_Intake is an 11-day-old, auto-qualified $75k Merlin Intake deal with one procurement evaluator (Jesse Frink), no discovery call, no champion, and Jaggaer already the incumbent; the only thing between us and a real deal is landing the first buyer conversation.

win_position.text: We are an unproven late-add in a three-vendor look at UNH: only Jesse Frink (Director of Contracts and Sourcing, an Evaluator) is on record, no buyer conversation has happened, and Jaggaer is already the incumbent (SF AIS_Why + OpportunityContactRole, 2026-06-25).
No economic buyer or budget owner is identified above Jesse, so we cannot yet tie Merlin Intake to a funded UNH priority (SF OpportunityContactRole, 2026-06-26).
Coupa and GEP both offer intake inside a full suite, giving UNH a one-vendor bundling path that a single-module Merlin Intake sale does not (SF Competitors__c, 2026-06-25).
The one real wedge: UNH runs Jaggaer and Workday, so Merlin Intake can be positioned as an additive AI layer rather than a rip-and-replace (SF AIS_Why, 2026-06-25).

Stage was moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 2026-06-26, one day after creation with no discovery in between, so 'Qualified' reflects a data step, not buyer validation (SF field history).

customer_commitment.text: The buyer has made no commitment: no meeting accepted, no dates named, no next-meeting requested (no Avoma calls, LastActivityDate null).
Jesse Frink is logged as Evaluator, not a champion or sponsor, and there is no evidence he will advocate or open doors (SF OpportunityContactRole).

deal_risk.text: This has the signature of an RFP courtesy inclusion: three competitors logged at creation, a procurement evaluator as sole contact, and a long runway with no engagement (SF Competitors__c + OpportunityContactRole, 2026-06-25).
If the first buyer conversation does not land in the next two weeks, the honest call is qualify-out or watch-list, not forecast (SF Tasks 2026-06-29: no inbound in 14 days).
